_meta.purpose: Recommend HF rerun points that most strengthen the posterior -> HF closure in the manuscript. No HF execution is performed by this file; it is a selection-only deliverable.
selection_principles: Prefer near-threshold points; information-per-HF-run is highest where posterior P(stress>tau) is closest to 0.5.
Prefer points where the posterior already has a prediction to close against (benchmark with stress_true or feasible-region with stress_post_mean).
Avoid redundancy in posterior risk level.
Target posterior physical consistency rather than dataset reproducibility; run HF at posterior mean, not at the original test-split parameter point.
For benchmark cases, prefer cleanly mixed chains with all 4 calibration parameters inside 90%CI, because the narrative claim is 'when inference is correct, the HF check confirms it'.

plan_A_minimum_3_points.description: Minimal closure set: one low-risk, one near-threshold, one high-risk. Adds 3 new HF runs to the 5 already archived.

candidates.why_this_point: The lowest-stress benchmark case whose 4-parameter posterior is fully inside 90%CI (cleanly-mixed MCMC). Running HF at this point's posterior mean should return a stress well below the 131 MPa threshold, validating the 'posterior narrows to low-risk' branch of the pipeline. Alternative candidates for this slot include case 5 row 10 (stress_true 102, but alpha_base is out of 90CI) and case 1 row 176 (stress_true 118.7, which is better reserved for Plan B).
candidates.recommended_rationale: At posterior_mean, the surrogate claim is 'stress at these 4 params is safe'. Re-running HF at the true point would only reproduce dataset row 433, which already exists; running at posterior mean gives new information about whether the posterior's inferred parameter vector is physically self-consistent.

candidates.why_this_point: The single most informative HF point in the entire candidate pool. Posterior P(stress>131) = 0.604 is the closest to 0.5 of any feasible-region case, and the posterior predictive interval [114.0, 157.8] straddles the threshold. If HF at posterior mean returns a stress in the mid-130s the posterior's threshold calibration is directly validated; if HF returns well below 131 or well above 140 the posterior is demonstrably biased near threshold.
candidates.recommended_rationale: Near-threshold cases are where the 131 MPa decision boundary matters. Running at posterior mean yields the single measurement most directly comparable to the posterior's 135.05 MPa prediction.

candidates.why_this_point: The feasible-region case whose posterior mean stress (177.13 MPa) matches its known true stress (177.89 MPa) most tightly of any P>tau=1.0 case (diff 0.76 MPa, below HF solver noise floor 0.11 MPa x ~7 tolerance). A HF rerun at its posterior mean should return ~177 MPa, closing the loop 'posterior -> HF -> same stress level' on a decisively high-risk point.
candidates.recommended_rationale: posterior_mean differs from benchmark_true by small amounts in each parameter; running at posterior_mean delivers a new HF data point (not a reproduction of dataset row 385) and tests whether the posterior's ~177 MPa prediction is physically reproduced.

plan_B_enhanced_5_points.description: Adds two points to Plan A: a second low/moderate (upper-low end of benchmark category) and a second high-risk (feasible-region case with posterior overshoot).

additional_candidates.validation_target: posterior discrimination within the low-risk category
additional_candidates.why_this_point: Stress 118.7 MPa is the highest in the low category, within 12 MPa of the threshold. Posterior is cleanly mixed (all 4 parameters in 90%CI) and distinct from Plan A case 3 (stress 103.7). Running this alongside case 3 tests whether the posterior + HF pipeline can reliably tell apart two low-risk operating conditions separated by 15 MPa.
additional_candidates.recommended_rationale: Same principle: new HF measurement at the posterior's inferred parameter vector, not a reproduction of dataset row 176.

additional_candidates.why_this_point: posterior_mean_stress (200.93) overshoots stress_true (193.53) by 7.4 MPa with a wide 90%CI of [178, 225]. This is a harder high-risk case than Plan A point 3. Running HF at the posterior mean answers a sharp question: if the posterior mean parameters really do produce ~200 MPa stress, the overshoot is physically genuine (just a different operating point, not a posterior bias); if HF returns closer to 193 MPa the posterior mean is biased toward higher stress. Either outcome is informative.
additional_candidates.recommended_rationale: Exactly where the posterior bias claim lives.

candidates_considered_but_not_selected.reason: low stress (102), but alpha_base is out of 90%CI; chain not cleanly mixed for this parameter, so closure validation becomes entangled with MCMC mixing diagnosis.
3 of 4 parameters out of 90%CI; too many failure modes, harder to attribute a HF rerun outcome.
near threshold (129 MPa) but alpha_base out of 90%CI; feasible_region row 250 is a cleaner near-threshold pick.
near threshold (129 MPa, clean chain). Close backup to the feasible_region row 250 pick, but lacks feasible_region's explicit P(stress>tau) value.
also near-threshold P>tau=0.65, posterior_mean 136.85 MPa; used as an alternative if row 250 cannot be rerun.
high-risk undershoot (post_mean 184 vs true 191). Similar diagnostic value to Plan B row 210 but with smaller error magnitude.

recommendation.if_budget_is_5_runs: Plan A plus row 176 (upper-low) and row 210 (high-risk overshoot diagnosis).
recommendation.which_plan_is_best_now: Plan A is sufficient to close the posterior -> HF narrative at the level the manuscript needs for a first submission. Plan B is strictly better for reviewer-robustness (it produces a second low-category point and a second high-risk point), but the marginal value of the 4th and 5th runs is smaller than the first three. If HF compute is cheap (each run is ~38 min on the current server), run Plan B. If compute is scarce or you want a fast turnaround, Plan A suffices.
required_inputs_per_case_before_running: All 4 calibration parameters (E_intercept, nu, alpha_base, alpha_slope) from benchmark_summary.csv at the selected case_idx, or stress-driven params computed from feasible_region.csv.
The 4 fixed non-calibration parameters (E_slope, SS316_T_ref, SS316_k_ref, SS316_alpha) at their nominal values from experiment_config_0404.DESIGN_NOMINAL.
